veteran.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoins and their issuers; it has no specific relevance to veterans, who are affected identically to the general public.

has_disability.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and has no provisions that affect people with disabilities differently.

student.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and has no particular relevance to students as such.

has_student_loans.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and digital assets; it has no bearing on federal student loan borrowers.

parent_of_minor.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuance and has no differential effect based on whether someone is a parent of a minor.

caregiver_of_adult.reason: The bill concerns stablecoin regulation and does not affect caregivers of dependent adults differently than anyone else.

homeowner.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuance and has no differential effect based on housing status.

renter.reason: Stablecoin regulation is unrelated to renting and affects renters no differently than others.

other.reason: The bill's effects do not vary with living arrangement.

citizen.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and reserve/compliance requirements; its effects do not differ by citizenship status.

permanent_resident.reason: The bill applies uniformly regardless of immigration status and does not distinguish permanent residents.

other_status.reason: Stablecoin regulation applies identically without regard to immigration status.

single.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and has no differential effect based on marital status.

married.reason: Stablecoin regulation applies identically regardless of marital status.

divorced.reason: The bill contains no provisions tied to marital status.

widowed.reason: No survivor or spousal provisions; marital status is irrelevant to this financial regulatory bill.

under_18.reason: The bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and applies to consumers regardless of age; minors are not a subject.

age_18_25.reason: Stablecoin regulation applies uniformly to users regardless of age, with no age-specific provisions.

age_26_49.reason: No age-dependent effect; the regulatory framework treats all adult users identically.

age_50_64.reason: No age-specific provisions; effects do not vary with this age bracket.

age_65_plus.reason: The bill contains no age-dependent terms; effects are identical across all ages.

under_30k.reason: This bill regulates stablecoin issuers and its consumer protections apply uniformly regardless of household income.

income_30k_60k.reason: The regulatory framework does not vary in effect by income level.

income_60k_120k.reason: No income-dependent effect; stablecoin rules apply the same across income tiers.

income_120k_250k.reason: No income-dependent effect from this stablecoin regulation.

income_250k_plus.reason: The bill's effects do not differ based on household income.

education.reason: The bill concerns stablecoin regulation and has no bearing on education work.

healthcare.reason: No connection to clinical or allied health occupations.

manufacturing.reason: No relevance to factory or industrial production work.

agriculture.reason: No connection to farming, ranching, or fishing.

transportation_logistics.reason: No relevance to drivers or logistics workers.

public_sector_first_responders.reason: The bill assigns extensive new duties to federal financial regulators (Treasury, OCC, Fed, FDIC, NCUA), directly affecting government employees at those agencies, though this is a narrow subset of the public sector.

professional_office.reason: The bill is a comprehensive financial-regulation regime directly governing stablecoin issuers, banks, accountants, compliance officers, and finance and legal professionals who are its explicit subjects.

retail_service.reason: No direct effect on retail, hospitality, or personal-service occupations.

technology.reason: Digital asset service providers, distributed ledger and blockchain developers, and crypto firms are directly regulated, with defined carve-outs for protocol and self-custodial software developers. Their business activities and legal exposure are firsthand subjects of the Act.

other.reason: No distinct occupation outside the listed buckets is directly targeted.

employee.reason: Stablecoin regulation applies to issuers and consumers regardless of employment status.

self_employed_owner.reason: The bill's stablecoin issuer requirements do not vary by a person's employment status; issuer regulation is a sector matter, not an employment-status trait.

unemployed.reason: No provisions relate to employment status.

retired.reason: No retirement or pension provisions here.

not_in_labor_force.reason: Irrelevant to those outside the labor force.

employer_plan.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoins and has no connection to health insurance coverage.

medicare.reason: No relation to health coverage or Medicare.

medicaid.reason: No relation to health coverage or Medicaid/CHIP.

marketplace_individual.reason: No relation to health insurance markets.

va_tricare.reason: No relation to veterans' or military health coverage.

uninsured.reason: No relation to health insurance status.

male.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and applies identically regardless of gender.

female.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and applies identically regardless of gender.

nonbinary_other.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoin issuers and applies identically regardless of gender.

lgbtq.reason: This bill regulates payment stablecoins and digital assets; it has no relevance to LGBTQ+ identity.
